paragraphs.text: In its current form Digital Fairness especially in the Context of Video Games but also in other Forms of Media is vastly Underrepresented at best. Rarely will one find a Game or Website these Days that is not Overloaded with Unnessesary Ads or Call to actions for Microtransactions. These Patterns are rarely if ever clearly Marked as Advertisements especially in the Context of Video Games and their Start Screens leading especially Younger Users to Klicking on these Advertisements unknowingly. Same is true for some Websites and while I understand that the EU cant regulate All Websites on the Internet as Only a small percentage are Hosted on EU Servers I'd Appreciate it if Especially Those Could be checked to be free of Dark Patterns and the Like. This is Especially My Hope for Websites whose target Audience includes Younger/Adolecent Users als it is not Uncommon to find even mildly Pronographic Ads on Websites whose Traget Demographic is not entirely for Adults.
